It’s always a disappointment when a sequel doesn’t quite live up to its predecessor, and unfortunately that’s what Ender Magnolia: Bloom in the Mist is, an incredibly well-polished but ultimately less interesting follow-up to Ender Lilies that, while still beautiful to watch an play, failed to draw me into its steampunk world.  The game is set decades after the previous instalment and follows a completely new set of characters. We play as Lilac, an attuner, part of a caste that can purify artificial life forms called homunculi who have been corrupted by the mists. Lilac calls upon the homunculi she has purified to fight in her place, from the enigmatic Nola to the dual pistol-wielding Yolvan.   The environments, characters, and animations are still absolutely breathtaking, with most areas filled with colour and detail. The steampunk aesthetic is used to good effect, with towering factory chimneys, robot assembly lines, and art-deco cities. The game is much more populated this time around too, with dozens of NPCs to interact with, including a handful that will send you out on fetch quests.  Unfortunately for me, Lilac and the steampunk biomes just didn’t hit the same way as Ender Lilies. Lily was portrayed as an incredibly vulnerable protagonist and that vulnerability made me care for the character. Watching her stumble and fall when she tried to dodge or flinch every time one of her spirits attacked was heart-wrenching. Lilac still carries some of that innocence, but is way more aggressive and confident.  I think I also preferred the general atmosphere of the first game, that was steeped in a dark fantasy melancholia, its biomes ruined remnants of towns destroyed by the Blight. Much like Hollow Knight or Blasphemous, this tragic decrepitude gave a sense of urgency to our protagonist’s quest, a harbinger of things to come if we failed.  Combat is still fun and challenging in Ender Magnolia, possible more challenging than in the first game, but I’m not sure some of the changes developers Adglobe & Livewire have implemented really work. I enjoyed hunting down minibosses in Ender Lilies to unlock their spirits, but now you only unlock eight homunculi, and the minibosses you defeat drop upgrade materials. It’s a subtle change, but in my opinion it sucks some of the fun out of collecting new attacks and abilities.  Speaking of abilities, you can now equip four instead of three, and while that didn’t bother me initially, I’ll admit it did become a bit finicky towards the end of the game. I ended up gravitating towards a ranged build, with two ranged homunculi, a AoE turret, and a melee attack. For the ranged homunculi to be effective you have to hold down two buttons constantly all while moving, jumping and dodging (so that’s two more buttons). Then the AoE turret is another button, and the melee attack another, for a total of six buttons.   If that wasn’t enough, you’ll also need to keep a finger free for your heals, and another couple of fingers to input the combo for your powerful SP attacks once the relative gauge is filled. It’s all a bit frantic and doesn’t always work as well as it should. Maybe it’s just because I’m old and dumb, but on some of the harder late game boss fights I lost once or twice due to not being able to dodge out the way fast enough because my finger was on another button. I’m probably in the minority here, but I think I preferred the slower, more melee-focussed combat of Ender Lilies.   Luckily, while the game may falter slightly in its combat, its traversal and exploration are still just as satisfying as before. You unlock a slew of traversal abilities from the get-go, including fast travel, and the map is excellent and clueing you in on unfound pathways and collectibles. Ender Magnolia is larger than the first game too, with several optional zones and bosses you’ll have to fight your way through if you’re gunning for the true ending.   Now I should be clear: Ender Magnolia is by no means a bad game, nor a bad soulsvania. It’s fantastic environments, beautiful animations and breezy exploration makes it easy for me to recommend. I wasn’t quite as enamoured with the combat and steampunk atmosphere, but fans of challenging boss battles will still find a lot to like here.

Great game, but worse then sequel.   I had great joy plying this game, but what makes me feel sad is seeing the regress. That is what I see compared to Ender Lilies.   I'll start with what is done good in the game. The exploration feels amazing, I was getting in the state of flow as I was roaming the map. The exploration is so good, that I was annoyed by presence of bosses, as it was preventing me from exploring further. They made a blunt move by giving OP mobility closer to the end of game, but still late game was challenging even with this OP abilities. Audio, visuals are amazing. One aspect this game improved from lilies is build making. Now we have less abilities, but they are much more practical and there no duplicate abilities like it was in lilies. Relics also give much more noticable change.   Combat itself is rather unpolished. Often I find the abilities of bosses that miss me, still hit. Some attacks are double punishing you, if you got damaged once, boss keep attacking and you cannot do anything but just see character die.   I can't tell what wrong in atmoshpere, but it gives impression that there is no style. The art done great, no doubt, but the game has no coherent style. It is rather a stew from incompatible styles that feels fake.   The biggest regress is in characters. In lilies each boss feels like a personal tragedy,  you sympathize them, and I still remember them. Here I don't really remember anything about characters, stories are cliche and presented raw.     Overall it's good game to spend time, but it will not give any emotional imprint and you'll forget it after finishing

Si te gustan los Metroidvania este juego es para ti, su arte, música, jugabilidad y todo lo demás es increíble! Mejoraron mucho el mapa el cual en la entrega anterior era bueno, pero un poco simple. La dificulta esta bien, no sentí que fuera muy fácil pero tampoco complicado, con un par de intentos puedes vencer a los jefes sin problemas, aunque el jefe final si estuvo complicado, cosa que me encanto!  No es necesario haber jugado Ender Lilies, puedes jugar este primero, pero en lo personal te recomendaría jugarlo primero antes que este, ambos son hermosos y una obra de arte! Además que así reconocerías ciertas referencias.  Espero con ansias futuras entregas, la historia podría abrirse a mas reinos como lo hizo con este, espero los desarrolladores lo sigan haciendo tan increíble y con tanto cariño como hasta ahora.

前作『ENDER LILIES』が好きだった人なら、十分おすすめできる作品です。 音楽、雰囲気、ストーリーはいずれも高い水準でまとまっており、シリーズらしい静かな美しさと切なさがあります。  前作『ENDER LILIES』が「死んだ世界へ花を手向けるゲーム」だとすれば、今作『ENDER MAGNOLIA』は「散る前の花を一緒に眺めるゲーム」だと感じました。 前作はすでに滅びた世界を進み、一握りの希望を胸に浄化していく物語でしたが、今作はまだ完全には死んでいない世界で、生きている人々との交流があります。 世界はいずれ絶望へ向かうかもしれない。それでも、それまでの時間を誰かと共に過ごす。 そうした「緩やかな滅び」の雰囲気があり、前作とは違う良さがありました。  戦闘面はかなり遊びやすくなっています。 装備や技の種類が増え、さまざまな組み合わせで戦えるようになりました。 死にスキルと感じるものも少なく、敵や状況に合わせて構成を変える楽しさがあります。 前作よりも戦闘の自由度は上がっていると思います。  一方で、パリィがかなり便利なため、極端に言えばパリィ中心でも進められてしまう場面があります。 これは難易度を下げ、より多くのプレイヤーが遊びやすいようにした意図だと思うので悪いとは感じませんが、やや強すぎる印象はありました。  気になった点は、メトロイドヴァニアとしての探索要素です。 本来であれば、新しいスキルを得ることで行ける場所が増え、「あそこに行けるようになった」という楽しさがあるはずですが、今作は中盤あたりで大体の場所へ行けるようになります。 そのため、後半にかけて探索範囲が段階的に広がっていく楽しさは、前作より薄く感じました。  また、どこからでもファストトラベルできる点も便利ではありますが、便利すぎることでマップを歩いて覚える感覚や、探索している実感は少し弱くなったように思います。 ただし、これも快適性を重視した調整だと思うので、単純な欠点というより好みが分かれる部分だと思います。  全体としては、前作よりも遊びやすく、戦闘の自由度も高く、作品として非常によくまとまっています。 ただ、前作のような孤独感、探索の重み、トゥルーエンドへ向かう強烈な達成感は『ENDER LILIES』の方が上だと感じました。  それでも今作には今作の良さがあります。 暗さ一辺倒ではなく、生きている人との交流があり、まだ残っているものを大切にするような雰囲気がある。 前作とは違う方向で、しっかりシリーズの魅力を広げている作品だと思います。  便利になったことは劣化ではなく、今作ならではの進化だと感じましたので、 次回作があるなら、『ENDER LILIES』の探索の重みと、『ENDER MAGNOLIA』の戦闘自由度・遊びやすさを合わせた作品に大いに期待したいです。
從一代過來，整體玩起來是舒服的，難易度適中雖然有些boss跟某些地圖有點小折磨但整體不會卡關到讓人很絕望，小地圖跟一代比起來也更清晰了；但在劇情跟整體氛圍部分我個人還是比較喜歡一代，一代那種衰敗淒美的風格實在很喜歡，二代感覺想複製但在這基礎上又沒有太多突破，新的機械生命體的設定也沒讓人留下太多印象，二代這次比較喜歡的音樂也是復刻一代的，稍微有點可惜。

《终焉之玛格诺利亚》像是把前作《终焉之莉莉》那套"泥泞孤绝的末世挽歌"拆开来，重新焊成一台更顺滑、更有人间温度、也更工业阴郁的阶级寓言机器。  《终焉之玛格诺利亚》把舞台从"覆灭后的废墟王国"搬进"烟尘之国"，一个魔法与蒸汽机械文明叠合的阶层城市，玩家扮演能救赎失控人造生命体的调律师莱菈可，一路从底层旧城区、血染研究所、庄严魔术学院到巨大工厂往上爬。它的叙事从"一个白衣女孩与幽魂同行"变成带配音、带同伴对话、能在幸存者据点看到人味与秩序崩坏痕迹的社会性悲剧，代价则是它确实比前作少了点那种"万物皆死只剩你与雨声"的纯粹孤绝感。部分主线支线的情绪堆叠也有虎头蛇尾之处。  《终焉之玛格诺利亚》的画面仍然是同级别的2D横版独立游戏中的精品，但审美重心从灰白废墟的"留白美学"转向更密更满的管道、齿轮与矿尘。视觉多样性更高，然而工厂、下水道、实验室区段也陷入一定的同质化，需要靠构图与前景细节硬扛辨识度。  《终焉之玛格诺利亚》的配乐最稳也最"Mili"，钢琴线条+空灵人声+电子碎片把"有毒雾气里的温柔"钉进场景，整体水准很高。不过与前作某些神级旋律与角色意象曲，本作配乐缺乏了那种直击感。  《终焉之玛格诺利亚》的玩法则是最能体现本作基于前作优化的地方。地图终于更像真实房间形状、区域收集完成会有提示、关键机动更直接绑按钮而非被特定灵魂或装备卡手，还给了难度选项与更友善的快回存档，探索阻力明显下降；虽然本作主角不论在机动还是战斗性能方面远优于前作，只是当人造生命体的系统被精简后(核心可用数远少于前作那26灵魂)，玩家会发现配装构筑的深度明显浅化，外加中段某些区段仍会出现数值跳变与"需要背板多于读招"的粗暴感，让游戏在"系统严密性"上依旧需要打磨。  《终焉之玛格诺利亚》不如前作那样"用孤独与哀伤让你心碎"，但更像个愿意与玩家携手前行的成熟续作，在人情味与阶级伤痕里把救赎这一主题从墓碑搬回了蒸汽与魔法的城市里。

A good sequel to Ender Lilies, with about the same length (took me ~16h for the first playthrough). Music, scenery and art design are again great. The gameplay and exploration is very similar to the first game. For the most part I'd say this game takes the things from the first game and improves upon them (I prefered the ability upgrade system and especially the map in this game).  If it doesn't improve them, it is a sidegrade. For example in the first game you could take two sets of three abilities with you on exploration. In this game you get a single set of four abilities to equip. The relic system is pretty much the same as in the previous game, but you also have other equippable items now. The counter for example is now an equipment instead of a relic, so it doesn't take up one of the relic slots.

I was a big fan of “Ender Lilies” and this game, its sequel, did not disappoint! You don’t have to play “Ender Lilies” first to enjoy “Ender Magnolia” though. It works perfectly as a stand alone game. But if you did play “EL” then you’ll appreciate it more. It takes everything I loved about “EL” and just improves upon them. The spirits you equip now no longer have limited number of uses before you have to rest and replenish them. Instead they work on a cooldown function which I liked a LOT more! The map, which wasn’t bad in “EL” is also improved. One great feature both games have is that if you haven’t found everything in a room it lets you know by the color of the room. Just like “Ender Lilies” that are a lot of spirits you can equip that give you different combat abilities. But in “EL” there were more than a few that I either didn’t like or didn’t find useful. That’s not the case in “Ender Magnolia”! Every combat ability is useful! Some might be better for certain areas or enemies but they are all so good I had a tough time deciding which ones I wanted to use! The game also lets you set you multiple loadouts so you can easily switch. I will say I found “Ender Magnolia” a little easier than “Ender Lilies”. But not by much! The game still gives a good challenge and has multiple ways in the options menu to increase or decrease the difficulty to make the game more accessible to all kinds of players. And just like “Ender Lilies” the world and soundtrack are gorgeous! Whereas “EL” was a dark, fantasy-inspired world, “EM” takes place in a slightly more advanced, industrialized world. The story and characters are excellent and so is the combat. Not just one of the best Metroidvania’s I’ve played but one of the best games I’ve played. I played it on Steam Deck & it looked and ran great on the Deck!

Sinceramente es un excelente sucesor a Ender Lilies, se nota que es una evolución de lo que nos dieron en el juego anterior.  Específicamente, tenemos una narrativa más desarrollada, diálogos, personajes y combate más trabajados, una mayor variedad de jefes y por sobre todo un mapa amplio.  Los jefes no son difíciles en si y muchas secciones secretas del mapa son bastante sencillos de descubrir, pero siendo honestos esto también era así en la entrega anterior y dicho elemento no es suficiente como para considerarlo algo negativo (al menos no para mí)  La banda sonora es impresionante, ya nos mostraron su alto nivel en Ender Lilies, y aquí no decepcionan, genuinamente me quede quieto en ciertas áreas solo para disfrutar la música.   Y por último, lo más característico es definitivamente su estilo artístico, lo puedo definir con una palabra: Precioso, cada escenario, los fondos, personajes y enemigos desbordan personalidad, no exagero al decir que podrías ver una imagen sin contexto del juego y sabrías decir como mínimo que es de uno de los ENDER, así de característico y bueno es.   Conclusión: Si te gustan los metroidvania este juego definitivamente te va a gustar, y si ya jugaste a Lilies entonces este te gustara aún más, desde mi perspectiva, por su precio y lo que ofrece, es un excelente juego que vale la pena probar.

Très bon métroidvania qui reprend les principes du premier jeu et les pousse un cran plus loin. Merci à la carte d'afficher les zones qui sont terminées et les barrières qu'on ne peut pas encore ouvrir, ce qui permet de s'y retrouver même après une longue pause.   Si vous avez aimé Ender Lilies, foncez. Si vous n'y avez pas joué, Ender Magnolia se suffit à lui-même et vous passerez juste à côté de quelques références à l'histoire du premier jeu.

If you were to ask me to describe this game in a single sentence I'd say this game is too good to be worth just one. Simply put, this game is utterly amazing. Everything about it is a near direct improvement of its predecessor (mind you, Ender Lilies was already pretty great in its own right with a few quirks). Only thing about it that may be worse is the story I guess but like also it's not like Ender Magnolia has a bad story even, just less somber and quiet. Although admittedly the fact this game's plot centers around something that was mentioned ONCE in Ender Lilies and never brought up again is just funny.  The story is great. It takes place in the Land of Fumes, a country torn apart and on the brink of collapse due to the blight and other societal failures. I especially appreciated Lilac, the protagonist of the game, having an actual active role in the story and being a fully speaking protagonist this time. As opposed to Lily in Ender Lilies, who was mute for no apparent reason. Speaking of the game's predecessor, if you haven't played that yet, you won't be entirely confused with this game's story. There's only 2 points where the game's plots intersect and one of those is left ambiguous if it was actually a intersection. I'm not gonna say what as they are both major spoilers for both games, but one of them hit like a truck. Only other thing worth of note is that they don't really explain the blight itself as a concept, as that had an entire game to fester and be explored (pun intended), but if you tell someone they're zombies you get like 70% of the way there.  Combat is a clear improvement over Ender Lilies already solid foundation. It's a a relatively simple set of abilities you can use, it feels so good using them well and creating a build that works for you. This time it's only major bosses that are your abilities, and you are able to upgrade all of them with 3 variants on each of them. This system rewards more finding a load out that works and pumping into that, then the extremely simple just pure damage or tank Ender Lilies combat system actually promoted. Bosses are a highlight just like last game, with all of them being extremely memorable, and pretty interesting to fight. All in all great combat.  Exploration is straight up the best I've seen in a metroidvania to date. Not even exaggerating. Usually you're on a relatively linear path forward, with a bunch of offshoot branches that reward you for being curious. With areas you can't access being technically open but being utterly useless to the player. Like yes you can technically go to fog canyon in Hollow Knight immediately after finishing green path, but there's nothing you can actually do there, so it's a waste of time until you actually have a certain ability. Ender magnolia, once you finished the tutorial the entire map is open to you (well half because this game is split into 2 acts but I digress). If you can enter an area you can finish it, and this means that curious folk play style isn't a waste of time. Combine that with the way it displays the map (more on that later), and you have the best exploration I've ever seen to date.  Completion is something that may not be appreciated by everyone, but my OCD ass is very thankful for this being extremely streamlined. I mean seriously, I think trying to 100% to Bloodstained is several people's joker moment. Any time you fully complete a room, it will turn blue on the map. This means there's no guessing about "where did I need that ability I have now?". Additionally, having the achievements being findable in the actual game menus and not relying on external servers is something I appreciate too. This was more or less in Ender Lilies, but it's not like I'm complaining about it not being changed when it worked great as is.  And finally the biggest selling point of Ender Lilies I think, atmosphere. I mean it was always going to be tough considering Ender Lilies is currently the best atmosphere in a game I've seen period, but Ender Magnolia is no slouch either. Everything about it oozes a world that's at its last breath. But it still has some hope unlike Ender Lilies never ending melancholy. Speaking of which, the OST just like Ender Lilies, is god tier. Every song is utterly amazing, and it oozes charm. Finally the graphics are much more complex than Ender Lilies, and are a lovely mix of the paper cut out art style in Ender Lilies, and something more traditional in an anime.  All in all, this is one of the best games I have ever played, and I can't recommend it enough

TITLE: Ender Magnolia Genre: Metroidvania/Souls-like Graphics: 5/5 Character Design: 4/5 Premise: 4/5 Gameplay: 9/10 Lore: 4/5 Story: 8/10 Progression: 10/10 Music/Sound: 9/10 Difficulty: Normal, besides the true final boss Gametime: 22ish hours for almost 100% (level ups and upgrades, basically just grinding things) Bugs: none Overall: 53/60 Recommended: Yes  Amazing sequel to Ender Lilies, these two are my personal favorite games in the genre. Quick snappy combat, great exploration, good level design, doesn't feel like they make the game difficult for the sake of being difficult like others in the genre (blasphemous).   The environments are really interesting as you go through levels of a city that have been impacted by something known as the "mutation". Much like the first game, a lot of the lore is contained in drops around the map and beating boss fights and seeing their memories. You end up finding yourself once again sympathizing with everything as Lilac journeys through this world.   the level design is pretty good too, lots of mobility, good ways to cheese fights if y ou want, and the ability to add some "self imposed" restrictions to make the game more difficult or quicker based on skill. My primary loadout ended up being pretty close to the first 4 weapon you acquire and using those the entirety of the game due to comfort and flexibility.   The customization of builds using the relics and bracelets is much better than the first game, lots of different ways to maximize your output per fight. Which i definitely did not take full advantage of and just slammed the same build for most of the game oops.   Really charming experience overall, much like the first game, with the worldbuilding and lore letting you feel something for the characters involved. Binary Haze has done it yet again and I do look forward to whatever happens next here.   Play it and suffer from skill issue!

终焉莉莉丝的续作（虽然剧情上没什么关联），相比前作来说，美术、动作流畅度、人物机能都有很大进度，超前期就可以拿到二段跳和冲刺。 地图探索部分，每一个小区域全收集后地图会高亮显示，很方便做全收集的查漏补缺。 战斗方面：有轮椅🤭，最喜欢可以用轮椅的战斗了 Very Good的类银佳作

[h3]操作[/h3] 手感依然很不错，战斗手感比起前作还要更好一些。而且还去掉了碰撞伤害，可以更专注于敌人的攻击动作上。 [h3]声效[/h3] BGM比起前作记忆点感觉要少一些，但是整体也十分优秀。中层街区和钢骨街的BGM个人非常喜欢。不同技能和打击的音效以及提示性的音效也非常完善。 [h3]剧情氛围[/h3] 比起一代的末日要热闹了不少，NPC和同伴的存在感也都很强，[spoiler] 可以看到前作的莉莉登场并充当了关键角色也是意外惊喜， [/spoiler]。氛围上来说我是更喜欢本作的。 [h3]流程体验[/h3] 本作采用了三层分层开放式的结构，每一层都可以在一定程度上自由选择探索方向，但是区别不大，也就是先往左还是先往右的差别，这导致其实整体的流程还是蛮线性的，每个关卡基本初见就能搜的很干净了，哪还去不了、需要啥能力地图上也可以很清晰的看出来，重复探索价值较低。很早就能拿到相当程度的探索能力，甚至二段跳和冲刺这种级别的能力在开局就可以获得，这在类银游戏里还挺罕见的。底层就能拿全前作就有的几种探索能力，跑图的体验整体还是比较流畅的，（搞得我以为中层就要打最终BOSS了） [h3]地图[/h3] 地图这块值得单独说明。本作的地图比起前作完全是质的飞跃，从信息量极低的方块连线直接变成了信息量爆炸的数据终端。地形、锁门(还有需要的能力)、能走的方向、还没开放的区域、道具收集情况、完成度...一览无遗，甚至可以随时随地打开地图传送回休息点，这给我干哪来了都，有些过于人性化了（褒义） [h3]关卡设计[/h3] 前面也提到了本作是三层分层开放式的结构导致的线性流程。关卡设计上也显然受到了影响，能去的去不了的地方一目了然，隐藏大多数都能靠直觉初见发现，轻量化了不少。底层的几个关卡的设计比起前作收敛了很多，基本上没有什么上强度的地方，连休息点都主打一个量大管饱。另外，深红森林这种完全没有地图设计纯靠传送点进不同的练功房打怪这种关卡咱们以后能别做了吗[spoiler]（什么？你是说这样的传送设计魔法学院还要再来一遍？）[/spoiler]。中层和上层的几个关卡相对来说开放了不少，强度也开始慢慢上来了，粪怪当然也是少不了的，就是不知道为什么休息点的数量也锐减了(，甚至上层统治区除了BOSS门前外有一个休息点外，这么大的一片区域就一个休息点？ [h3]战斗[/h3] 技能、三种不同的装备、遗物可以组合出不少不同流派的战斗方式，没有了碰撞伤害还给了弹反，更没有死亡掉落，主角性能这块是稳稳的接住了；但是你极短的受击无敌又弥补了这一点，被BOSS、小怪、机关还有机关枪一样的弹幕两下直接带走在后期都是家常便饭，只能说熟悉的感觉又回来了。但是话又说回来了，本作的难度是可以完全自定义的，大家完全可以按照自身体验随时回休息点进行调整。 [h3]总结[/h3] 和前作一样有着高水准音乐和美术的优秀类银作品，难度不高且有相当多便利的人性化设计，甚至可以说适合作为新人体验该类型作品的入坑作，推荐。（虽然我个人更喜欢前作） PS：能把这一作的地图机制做个更新带回一代去吗，我太需要了。

Partiamo da un presupposto: il gioco ha assoluto bisogno di una serie di patch strutturali. Bisogna patchare assolutamente il damage imput. non è accettabile che un singolo colpo, anche del mob più scemo, tolga una valanga di vita come qui. Parliamo anche di oneshot, nel caso dei boss. Se lo avete voluto, siete dei pazzi da galera. Bisogna patchare assolutamente le hitbox. I colpi dei nemici ti fanno danno anche se si è evitato e si è oltre l'area dell'animazione. E, in generale, non si capisce mai quando lo si schiva o quando lo si becca. Qui ho avuto proprio l'impressione che il gioco decidesse arbitrariamente di volta in volta. Bisogna patchare assolutamente i tempi di parry e schivata. Non si può subire danno perchè, nonostante le pressioni siano veloci e a tempo coi colpi, le animazioni sono lente e ti fanno trovare in inferiorità di possibilità rispetto ai colpi. Mi spiego meglio: raffica da sei colpi, voi premete a tempo su tutte e sei ma il personaggio ne fa solo tre. Le altre tre prendete danno. Inoltre, stesso attacco, stesso tempo di pressione... risultati diversi. Gli avversari, guardacaso, cominciano un po' troppo spesso l'animazione di spostamento o un'animazione normale (hanno hyper armor) nel preciso momento in cui sferri un colpo speciale, facendolo andare a vuoto. Tutto questo, nei boss diventa devastante. Le bossfight sono qualcosa di scandoloso, indecenti, frustranti, noiose e ingiustamente troppo punitive. E la cosa che mi fa ridere è che, tranne quella finale (si dovrebbe parlare un'ora solo di quella), non sono mai veramente un ostacolo.  Quella finale.... oddio. Una bossfight messa lì proprio per rendere il tutto estremamente frustrante. Puoi tranquillamente fare bene tutto, no hit, prendi due colpi alla fine e sei morto. Un caos assurdo, imput lag come se piovesse, aoe del 90% dello schermo, tempi di parry e schivate che sembrano cambiare a suo piacimento. Insomma, il classico esempio di chi doveva fermarsi prima e ha rovinato un'esperienza per arroganza. E per inseguire questo mito tossico con cui mamma From ha rovinato il mondo del gaming... "deve essere DIFFICILEEEEEE". Anche se non lo sanno fare, ecco perchè fare le scelte di cui prima. Sono incapaci di creare moveset difficili ma puliti, impegnativi ma onesti. E fanno ste cagate. Il peggio del peggio. Il gioco è pure bello, nel suo complesso, però viene rovinato da tutti questi problemi. Se dovessero fare la patch che ho elencato all'inizio, allora vale la pena giocarlo. Diversamente, rischiate di arrivare in endgame e rovinarvi tutta l'esperienza perchè questi hanno deciso così. A voi la scelta.

전작 [엔더 릴리즈]에 이은, 메트로배니아식 플랫포머 소울(?) 게임 [엔더 매그놀리아].  전작에 비해 적들의 난이도가 엄청 쉬운 편이며, 플탐 15시간 정도를 갖고 있다. 보스들의 패턴을 파악하고 클리어하는 소울류식 게임이지만, 소울류라고 하기엔 난이도가 상당히 쉽다. 어느 보스도 (막보포함) 4트 이상을 넘긴 적이 없이 클리어가 가능하고, 길바닥에서 죽으면 타 소울류게임과 달리 재화를 잃는다거나 시체를 남기는 정도의 패널티가 전-혀 없다. 일본 개발사 게임이어서 인지, 한글번역이 매우 매끄럽고, 부담감이 없는 플레이를 제공하기에 가볍게 소울느낌의 플레이를 즐기고 싶은 게이머라면, 추천하고 싶다. 전작을 한 플레이어들도 꼭 해보길 바란다.

It's a good enough Metroidvania, especially given the price point. Took me 17 hours or so to pretty much 100% the game (all that would be missing is buying everything from the one single vendor, which ... nah, I'm good.)  If you played Ender Lillies, this is pretty much the same with a different setting and some minor variations, like skills no longer costing anything, which means you can basically play as a pew-pew warrior as well, or poison mist enemies to your heart's content.  That being said ... I think Lillies was the better game. By a slim margin at least. More interesting boss fights, a story I actually somewhat cared about and got into, just more exploration in general I think. Here, you're almost ... railroaded. There is one optional area. One. And you get 3 skills for each "homunculus" you defeat, while each unique enemy in Lillies had its own boss fight and skill, with a more general upgrading system. In Magnolia about half the skills require specific upgrade items.  There is also no "secret boss" or ending. You fight the final boss, and that's it. Either in the easy version or the hard version ... for some reason. There is no lore reason why he'd be weaker in Ending A ... the fight is fun, mostly, fair, mostly ... it's also the only fight (or maybe there's 2) with any sense of challenge. Lillies was harder BY FAR ... especially since you could wander into very hard bosses by just exploring. Lillies also had a super tough secret boss and final final boss ... not sure if they just didn't want to do that here, ran out of time ... or money.  The game is still good, but I wouldn't necessarily recommend it over Lillies ... But that means I'm giving it a "neutral" rating, which sadly doesn't exist on Steam. Since I don't think it's better than the game that came before ... I think that is wasted potential.

第一眼卖相一般的大作了。~学习版后来补票。 游戏系统挺有意思的，CD流、高频率输出、高爆发输出、自动输出流派很多，可以混搭，打起来很有意思。地图超级无敌霹雳大，在没有垃圾跑图的前提下差不多能打20个小时左右，很不错。交互拉满……不看攻略完全不影响游戏体验，交互体验拉满，比起很多所谓的看了攻略D级难度，不看攻略各种恶心的S的硬核银河城好多了。 唯一的缺点是BOSS战难度偏低，实际上难度低的原因一个是①BOSS门口的存档点，大幅降低了试错的时间成本，更容易和BOSS战斗试装备和技能战斗；②就是数值偏低，实际很多BOSS我都是数值碾压过去的，都没有机会和BOSS做交互（其实把BOSS的伤害提高，降低容错就会好不少）。 我觉得探索的难度刚好合适，不高不低，BOSS的数值偏低：如果能把一周目的探索作为普通难度；BOSS作为困难难度我觉得体验会更好，也更容易有记忆点。 这个和剑星是一样的，很多人说剑星难度低就是一周目开的普通模式，普通模式BOSS又太简单了，困难模式在大地图的叠怪之前容错太低，会有答大幅的挫败感；所以我玩剑星一周目的时候探索大地图我就开普通；打一本道和BOSS就切困难乐趣就多了很多。 （PS：困难不是为了装逼，降低容错才更容易和BOSS有交互，就像打格斗游戏一样--实力接近才有意思，虐菜几次还好，多了就没意思了）这可能也是这个游戏记忆点不强的原因吧？（其实1代我感觉也是这样……好作品，但是记忆点不多-更多的是虐心的剧情作为记忆点的。）

前作プレイ済。今作全実績解除済です。  前作より色々快適になってました。 まずスキル系の回数制限がなくなり、仲間キャラを積極的に使えました。 攻撃エフェクトも前作は判定が分かりにくかったですが今作はしっかりしたエフェクトです。 マップの境界線が表示され、探索してる感や達成感が増しました。 マップ表示も何のスキルがあれば通れるみたいな親切表示があります。  音楽は前作同様最高ですし、一部前作以上の曲もありました。  ここからイマイチだった点ですが、世界観は前作の方が好みでした。 今作はNPCが結構いたりするのですが掘り下げが少なくあまり感情移入が出来なかった事も原因かと思います。 NPCがいる事自体は個人的にはプラスなのですがマップの特性か（おそらく攻略マップ順序を間違えた）隊長クラスの人間が出てきてすぐに倒れて死んでしまって「？？？」となりました。 よく分からない悪の親玉が結構クローズアップされていましたがそれよりはライラック周辺の関係を掘り下げて欲しかったとは思います。  前作はNPCがほぼいなく、シンプルながらそれぞれのキャラの生い立ちがはっきりして分かりやすい 今作はNPCがたくさんいるが掘り下げが少なく、感情移入がしにくい  まぁそれでもいい作品に間違いないので前作をプレイ済の方にはおすすめします。 前作未プレイの方は前作からプレイされる事をオススメします。

듀얼 센스 패드로 플레이  초회 엔딩 + 맵 100% 까지 20시간 뉴게임 + 회차 진행 및 보스 러쉬, 상점 구매 & 강화 마무리까지 37시간에서 종료에서 작성  엔더 릴리즈 이후  메그놀리아를 한 평가가 들어 있습니다.  ====================================================  엔더 시리즈는 메트로베니아 장르 작품 중 정말 쉽게 플레이 할 수 있는 게임이라고 생각합니다.  메트로베니아의 정석이라고 할 수 있는 맵 탐방의 능력 해금과 숨겨진 공간 같은 탐험을  너무 어렵지 않게  체험 할 수 있게 짜여 있습니다.  그리고 후속작 이라는 이름에 맞게 전체적인 편의성도 개선되어   메트로베니아를 해볼까 하는 사람들에게 추천을 합니다.  전투 측면에서도 능력을 어떻게 세팅 하나에 따라 근거리 & 원거리, 수동&자동으로 원하는   방향으로 플레이도 가능합니다.   단, 이런 점들이 메트로베니아를 다수 접한 사람들에겐 조금 아쉽게 다가 올 수 있습니다.   너무 쉽다고 느낄 수도 있고 플레이 타임이 아쉽다고 느낄수도 있다고 생각합니다.  그래도 미려한 그래픽, 게임에 맞는 잔잔한 OST, 불편하지 않은 조작감 등  잘 만든 수작 게임이 맞습니다.  ====================================================  개인적으로 느낀 단점이라면 스토리 부분이 엔더 릴리즈에 비해 아쉽다는 점과  (노트, 힌트, 컷씬으로 유추 할 수 있는 스토리 라인이 전작 보단 부족하다고 느꼈습니다.)  뉴게임+ 에서 추가되는 점이 적들 패턴 추가(적음), 추가로 할 수 있는 것이 보스 러쉬 뿐이라는 점이 있네요.  (강화++도 유품 쪽만 있고 장비 & 스킬 쪽은 없어서 부족함을 느꼈습니다.)

I loved the interconnectivity with Ender Lilies so much, I knew this game would have some kind of lore tie-ins but I didn't expect the breadth of it nor how compelled I was reading every page I found. First session of playing I was a bit confused at the amount of movement tech they throw at you almost immediately, I was already swimming and double jumping which then led into long-dashing and ground slams. But by the mid-game I started getting more flow between the upgraded/advanced bits that felt better paced out to me, and the power curve on how much you can take vs how much you can dish out was definitely requiring more thought than just combo spamming things to death.  First frustrations with bosses was fine, they were tough to read but not impossible and can be figured easily with multiple attempts if not just done first try. There was only one singular hidden item that I had to actively Google search to solve and it was definitely not a fault of my own as it was essentially a pixel-perfect positioning just to nail it.  As before in Ender Lilies, I loved the endings and having Mili for the OST fit so well in a world of somber disrepair and magical inflections. Will definitely be doing the wrap-up grind to get money for the final two shopping achievements for 100%.

全成就完结撒花，mili的音乐依旧好听，战斗及探索的体验都还不错，总体来说是款不错的类银河恶魔城游戏。 与一代相比而言，战斗的手感提升很多；取消了碰撞伤害，对玩家更为友好；小地图的显示不再是一代的小方块，地图显示更有辨识度了；主副技能都有三种形态，技能选择更加丰富；取消了一代技能的次数限制，使得战斗的节奏更加紧凑；背景音乐依旧保持保持高水准，就是跟一代的曲子相比，没有那么令我印象深刻，但总体来说还不错。 剧情方面感觉到后期推进的有些仓促，不知道是不是后期赶工的原因，剧情刻画没有一代那种末世救赎的苍凉感让我感触那么深刻，也许是本作活人有点多了。本作中也会出现一代的主角莉莉，相遇时会响起一代主题曲，情怀拉满了。 我在游玩的时候选择的是自定义难度，前面四个难度选项拉到顶了，开了敌人双倍血量伤害、增加敌人的攻击频率等，难度拉满了，当时就想看看难度拉满了体验咋样，给自己上点强度。在这个难度下刚开始时还不太适应，血量感觉就是假的一样，随便被敌人攻击打到就是掉大半血到甚至直接暴毙了，后面熟练了就感觉可以接受，比玩魂游还刺激。后面在这个难度下全收集全成就我打得时间就会长一些，有些地方的推图会有些恶心人，点名批评最后的上层区域给的篝火少，中间堆怪多，跑图久，体验很不好；然后有些boss要练习很久，boss战中令我印象深刻的是魔女，那个魔女召唤出来的回血机器人，开了敌人血量翻倍后要打很多下才能打死，打不死就一直回血，当时卡了好久，还有那个工厂的大摆锤，被它那个机械臂打到一下就直接暴毙，几乎没有容错率，以及最终形态的吉尔罗伊，打真结局的四个阶段，伤害很高，如果没有带减伤护符的情况下被飞剑、贴身的延迟爆炸命中，那就直接没了，还有他三四阶段几乎零帧起手的防反也是难顶，被他防反震一下直接没了。当时玩近战的镰刀被打破防了，简直防不胜防，打他的攻击还不能快，攻击得一下一下的按，听到打他的声音不对，就得立刻停手闪避，注意力得高度集中才能躲得掉，练习了好几个下午才最终拿下。如果是玩轮椅的远程流派那就只需要在安全距离一直输出就完事了，那要好打很多。 总体来评分的话，满分10分我会给8.5分，喜欢2D类银河恶魔城的、喜欢一代白毛团子莉莉的粉丝值得入手体验。

就在同一天，我全成就通关了前作，兴致勃勃打开续作，但是就两个多小时的体验下来，我有点失望      先讲讲优点，或者说，优化，会更准确些。画面精致了好多，地图显示了详细内容，还会自动标注可拾取物，取消了碰撞伤害，可以走A，跳跃手感和攀爬的吸附手感好了许多，可以自定义难度。      接下来是我不满意的点。比起前作主角充满宿命感的设定与目标，本作就略显普通，玩到现在矿坑打通了都还没搞清楚主角想干嘛，击败完boss后，也缺乏前作的感动。      操作上，移动中转向会有种粘滞感，似乎有点惯性，前作也好像有这种感觉，但没这么明显，冲刺好像比前作短很多，而且移速似乎更慢      比起前作没个活人，还带点克系的氛围来说，这作引入了许多活人npc，多了点rpg要素，搞得我有点不适应，刚打开游戏在想二段跳会是哪个boss给我，这作会不会把二段跳后置的时候，路边一个npc把二段跳和冲刺同时发给我了，就……感觉很廉价，缺少羁绊的感觉，我更喜欢把他们换成x10x30x800甚至x100的渣渣，对了，说到渣渣，本作的强化系统的强化道具分了好几种，有卡进度的嫌疑，同时，又另外引入了货币系统，搞得很麻烦。      接下来是重头戏，我最想吐槽的地方来了，上面说有个优点是移除了碰撞伤害，但同时，这同样是一道限制。在玩前作的时候，你会发现，因为碰撞伤害的存在，boss不会把你逼进角落，这点在打老将的时候比较明显，本作跟老将对标的就是加拉鲁，矿坑的boss，技能类似，连打完给的模组都一样。     但！是！呢！谁家boss疯狗一样复读接复读接跳你脸上复读，二阶段在地图中间拉石都没地方躲，外加受伤没有无敌帧，特别容易化身小馋猫被直接带走，三阶段就更甚了，攻击欲望进一步提升的同时变成了几乎全屏的粪招复读，打到我怀疑人生     拜托，我可是今天才全成就前作欸，手感火热着欸，怎么会被一个前期boss搞成这样，实在是被整红温了，调到简单难度以后，再把频率从0.75调成0.5才打过，与此同时，我并没有产生前作打完boss的成就感，只剩下对未来疯狗boss的担忧      我想缸子了，我想水魔女了，你把莉莉还我，我要回家: (      二编踩转赞，轮椅妹和锁子哥真好用巴，缸子和水魔女别联系我了，我怕他们误会

This game was played in its entirety on a MacBook Air M1 8gb ram 7c gpu running MacOS Sonoma. There were no crashes throughout my time with the game. All achievements unlocked at their appropriate times. I encountered a fairly severe memory leak that required me to relaunch the game every two hours or so. It was likely due to my configuration.  Every review is going to compare this game with its predecessor, Ender Lillies. I'll try to avoid doing that too much, beyond a cursory better than/worse than for each category. Overall, I liked this game more than Ender Lillies. It has higher highs, but also lower lows, than Lillies.  The Positives: The soundtrack is the absolute best part of the game. Nearly every track is an absolute banger. I found myself not wanting to leave certain areas just to listen to the music longer. Similarly, the audio effects are top-notch. Every weapon gets its own suite of sounds, and the way weapons can interact audibly is very neat and interesting. Lillies was excellent in this regard as well, so I feel like this category is a draw.  The graphics and animations are incredibly strong. The levels are varied and take the player through quite the plethora of biome conditions. Animations are excellent, and every boss has wonderfully emotive stances and weapon abilities. Some are very fast, but there are no surprise attacks here. You are given the opportunity to learn each attack's "tells", and the game's outstanding quality of life (more on this later) helps in this regard by allowing you to view any enemy and their attacks from any save point.  Many players complained about Lillies drab/brown color scheme. I disagree with that assessment and find Lillies to be a well drawn and animated adventure, though I still prefer Magnolia's world. Magnolia wins this one.  A special shout out has to be made for the character designs in this game. They are nearly peerless (giving Nine Sols a run for its money!) and that includes both friendly and enemy designs. Standouts include No. 7, Nola, Yolvan, and Lorna.  The story is incredibly enjoyable. It does fall into the fairly common trap (at least these days) of trying to be vague enough to not offend anyone, while leaving the ending open enough that any sequels are not hampered. But, it directly expands upon the universe created in Lillies, and deepens the overarcing mystery that is present in both games. The best parts of the story, for me, are the chats you can have at save locations. Every character you recruit is given time in the spotlight, and they are all interesting and entertaining stories, without exception. The quality of the story is very high in both games, so I'll give this one a draw.  Gameplay is very good but standard for a metroidvania. Controls are crisp and responsive and I never felt floaty or slow. You are given some very strong powers right at the start, so I was curious how the rest of the unlocked powers would unfold. There are some very interesting ones to find in this game, but not all of them are incredibly fun to use. The protagonist is complex to control nearly from the outset, so most of the newer powers are either button combos, or hold and release style powers. Those are not my favorite, especially in combat, though they do all work as advertised. I felt like Lillies had a tighter control over the powers, so I'll give this one to the original.  The difficulty in this game is very deceptive. It starts out very easy. Too easy in fact! Lillies was an easy game throughout, and I was worried that Magnolia would follow the same path. Thankfully, the game ramps up in difficulty quite nicely as you progress. Quiet a few of the latter half of the bosses required multiple attempts, though none were rage-inducing (I enjoyed Velgrynd!). This was an easy win for Magnolia.  Quality of Life (hereafter qol) is normally not something I have to talk about at length for a review. It is definitely warranted here though. Simply put, Magnolia has some of the best qol of any MV game (of any game, really) that I've ever seen. The map colors each room as completed, everything not picked up is auto-marked on the map (and you're given tons of map markers if you need them), you can teleport back to a save spot at any time, there are no corpse runs, you can skip (and even auto-skip!) all cutscenes once they have been viewed at least one time, and the list goes on and on. They really made a huge effort to give the players a massive amount of qol in this game. Magnolia easily wins this category, which is why the next bit is so perplexing...  The Negatives: Simply put, I don't think Magnolia is tuned very well, at least in this current version (1.1.0). Like its predecessor, it does not require a great deal of backtracking. Most areas can be 100% completed except for a handful of locations, and most of the time they all require a single new power. You get that power, you teleport back to this location, and finish the handful of holdout collectibles, and that area is completely done (no reason to ever return). I mention this because maybe the developers are expecting more backtracking and confusion. Regardless of the reason, I found myself at the end of the game and with very little currency, and tons of things to still buy! I had to grind for a few hours to get enough to complete all of the achievements. I was not able to get enough scrap to enhance more than a third of (cheaper) relics, and I was only able to get enough fragments for a few outfits and some artwork. Even with all of the grinding, I was never able to afford any of the "second batch" of relic slot points. Since none of that was required for achievements, I felt that it was ok, but I would have liked to get more enhancements and extras throughout my run. I did keep the difficulty at its default for almost all of the game. Lillies wins this category easily as this was not an issue in that game in the slightest. Another point of contention here is that each update for the game has patched out the most well-known farming locations/methods of the time. Why?? It's a single player game, why are you forcing us to either grind for hours or go into an NG+ to complete the collection achievements?  My final nitpick negative is that this game is almost too polished. The original gave you a lot more freedom in your builds (but to the point where you could hurt your chances of completion). I really enjoyed the tense decision making about what abilities got enhancements in the first game. Here, you get enough to max everything. Some players may enjoy this approach more, but I prefer the near infinite freedom that the first game allowed you.  That's my entire review. Thanks for reading this far!

엔더 릴리즈 엔딩을 못본 이로서 매그놀리아를 할까말까 많이 고민했었다. 엔더 릴리즈 때는 아름다운 브금, 비주얼이 게임을 시작하게 했던 요인이었는데 반해 중도하차를 한 이유는 당시의 나에게 엔더 릴리즈의 난이도가 너무 어려웠기 때문이었다.  많은 사람들이 지적한 것처럼 길찾기가 어려운 것도 한몫했다.  엔더 매그놀리아는 이런 부분들이 개선되었고, 그 결과 엔딩을 볼 수 있긴 했다. 근데 엔더 매그놀리아에서도 리트가 요구되는 구간은 많긴 했다. 스토리를 보기 위해 난이도를 낮추었음에도 불구하고 딜이 쎈 보스들도 많았으며, 패턴도 생각보다 까다로웠다. 막보는 오히려 쉬웠고, 매그놀리아를 플레이하며 막혔던 보스는 기계 시리즈 중 첫번째로 나온 상태이상작열과 둔기류 공격을 하는 보스였다. 거기서만 한 60번 리트했던 것 같다.  암튼 내가 말하고 싶은 결론은  똥손도 스토리를 보기 위한 인권이 있다!는 것이다. 난이도를 낮출거면 제대로 낮추는 것도 옵션의 하나로 구현되었으면 좋겠다.  아, 엔더 릴리즈에서 막힌 구간은 미친 기사 뭐라는 그.... 늑대같이 생긴 놈이었음 그럼 이만 빠이

Worthy sequel. Gameplay and QoL were improved (lack of contact damage is big), but story and atmosphere were weaker. Individually I still prefer Ender Lilies, but taken as a whole both games elevate each other lore and story wise. In Ender Lilies you are shown in graphic and tragic detail what happens when Blight winds and consumes all, so while you don't see the full extent of that in Ender Magnolia (only beginnings and there is still hope) you are fully aware of what's at stake and the final result that not even characters themselves are aware of save one

The story and the characters weren't as engaging as they were in Ender Lilies, I struggled to connect to them emotionally. Was also a little cliche at times. Nevertheless, the gameplay was nice and the map exploration was pretty fun. I first questioned the choice to make the map so detailed but soon realised it was necessary for some hidden areas. Overall, 6.9/10?

比前作更像一个合格的规整的类银，所以我游玩的体验其实是好很多的。相比莉莉，这作取消敌人碰撞伤害、可随时传送任意存档点、地图更详尽便捷便于全收集、操作手感有一定改善、人物性能增强、强度较高且没有次数限制的远程技能，都让推图的过程更简单更舒心了，我给出好评也是基于这些还不错的游戏体验。  但是玩起来不舒服的点依然有不少： 1.地图上每个大区域没有颜色区分，统一显示为蓝色（全收集）和灰色（未全收集），打开大地图看起来不直观。地图标记数量只有15个太少了。 2.地图设计问题，上层统治区的超冲解谜部分还能说得上有一些设计感，相比之下深红森林和魔法学院不明确标注出口的单向小传送门造成的迷路和重复跑路只有让人红温的感受。 3.敌人数值问题，我初见全程使用困难难度，但据我了解普通难度数值问题是一样的，倒不是难度有多高，但依然是不合理的，玩家血条和装备所给予的防御属性的成长并没有带来任何直观的正收益，中期开始大量小怪和boss对玩家都是两三刀秒，更有数不胜数的多段伤害判定加上主角没有受击无敌，稍有不慎被连击秒杀也是常有的事，这就导致中后期体验越来越差，推图始终处于谨慎提防小怪的状态中，没有其他类银越到后期越上天入地无所不能的体验。 4.boss设计不如前作，大部分boss招式简单没有记忆点，全靠堆砌血量和伤害数值撑场面，只有七号这一个boss我比较喜欢（不包括他前面的那三个小boss），诸如魔女琉赛、魔导工厂的维尔格林德、b结局吉尔罗伊这些boss让我记住的点都是数数又值值，用高伤害、密集弹幕、秒人大招等降低玩家容错，boss本体在场上多动症一样从屏幕最左边跑到最右边，近战伤害较难打出，逼着玩家用远程技能和轮椅构筑与boss对狙，打赢这些boss并没有成就感。 5.冲刺手感疑似不如前作，距离较短，贪刀时经常冲刺不出来挨打，不知道是不是冲刺无法及时打断攻击的缘故。而且在小怪和boss经常释放弹幕攻击的环境下，冲刺的收益不如使用技能或外壳弹反，但是敌人伤害数值高又使得弹反风险高，没法熟练的弹反还不如冲刺躲避。 6.货币有很大的缺口，商店东西要价太高，没有任何时刻资源是够用的，商店的很多东西作用不大还贵，在通关后还需要专程去刷资源来买这些无意义的贵物。与其卖那么多我买不起的魔导管，不如把魔导管上限和遗物普遍占用的格子数都削一下。另外解锁设定集全部图片所需的碎片量也过分高昂，困难模式1.57倍的碎片掉落依然面临10w+的碎片缺口，不想给人看设定集可以不给，以为大家都有兴趣反复打你那个bossrush刷碎片吗。  作为莉莉的续作，玛格诺利亚的音乐美术依旧顶尖（音乐我在玩的时候没怎么注意到，但单独去听ost的时候发现还是很优秀的），剧情交代得不太清楚，角色塑造和氛围营造就差很多了，虽然莱拉克可以说话可以和队内的同伴聊天，但这作对于主角乃至所有角色的塑造都没有前作莉莉深入，每次回忆的过场动画都云里雾里，剧情演出很少有打动人的点，完全没有莉莉那种后劲很足的感觉，所有剧情深度和想表达的东西都仿佛轻飘飘没有落地，按理说这个承接前作的污秽的世界观可以承载很多有深度的表达，在这个更有活人感的世界里可以讲出独属于烟尘之国的故事，可以深度聚焦米利厄斯与弗罗斯特、人与人造生命体、上层与下层在面对污秽时的冲突对立与统一，或者用群像刻画出更富有希望的世界，实际上（含剧透）[spoiler]b结局里人造生命体成为了和人一样寿命有限的存在共同迎接美好未来这是一个很有意思的ending[/spoiler]，但呈现出来的却是张力不足草率收场的效果，不好说到底是欠缺了哪一个点才导致这部在剧情和氛围上的不尽如人意，不如说欠缺的实在是太多了。

作为《终焉的莉莉》续作，《终焉的玛格诺利亚》整体表现可圈可点，既有传承前作的高光，也有自身的优化与遗憾，游玩体验十分扎实。 配乐依旧延续了前作的顶级水准，旋律动人且贴合场景氛围，无论是探索时的舒缓旋律，还是战斗时的激昂节奏，都能精准烘托情绪，堪称游戏的灵魂亮点。画面风格则与前作形成鲜明对比，褪去了前作的史诗厚重感，整体色调更为明亮清新，视觉上更显轻快，虽少了几分氛围感，却也带来了更舒适的长时间游玩体验。 地图设计相比前作有了质的飞跃，指引清晰、布局合理，即便不依赖攻略，也能顺利完成全收集，彻底摆脱了前作可能出现的 “满世界找遗漏道具” 的折磨，探索体验大幅提升。 战斗系统的优化最为明显，手感相比前作流畅了太多，操作反馈更精准，且取消了令人诟病的碰撞伤害，避免了 “贴怪就掉血” 的烦躁体验。主角的性能堪称逆天，拥有极为丰富的移动与战斗手段 —— 跳跃、技能位移、普攻衔接移动、空中冲刺、空中防御、二段跳，多种操作可灵活组合，甚至在游戏中期，就能凭借这些技能组合，抵达原本需要后期超冲、上冲能力才能解锁的区域，探索自由度拉满。更贴心的是，游戏内置难度调节设置，即便你是动作苦手，也能通过调整难度轻松应对 BOSS，不会因卡关影响游玩节奏。 队友塑造方面，终于摆脱了前作 “只活在回忆里” 的尴尬，在各个休息点，大概率会触发与同伴的对话互动，让队友不再是冰冷的背景板，多了几分 “活人感”。但遗憾的是，这些互动仅局限于简单对话，并未像传说系列那样做成专属小剧场，无法深入展现队友的性格、背景与羁绊，导致队友整体塑造不够立体，只能算是 “有了存在感，却没有记忆点”。

Ender Magnolia is a beautiful game. If anyone loved Ender Lilies, I have no doubt you'll have a similar feeling playing this game as well. The music is fast-paced to match the game's setting, the combat is fast so there's more engagement with enemies, and exploring the map has become easier but still challenging. The developers improved the mini-map system from the last game so you wouldn't get lost so easily trying to find out how to get to the next part of the story.   I would say that some bosses can be unfair such as some running away most of the time or having too much health compared to later bosses but the equipment in the game can deal with some of those problems. I do like the story of Ender Lilies more while I had a slightly hard time understanding the story of Ender Magnolia. Finally, if you haven't played Ender Lilies, it's not mandatory to play it to understand Ender Magnolia although I do recommend playing it to get a better sense of the world's setting.

一頻りやったため感想  前作が良かっただけに残念といった感じ。 前作の欠点だった「そんなん分かるかアホ」というアイテム回収がなくなった。 代わりにダークな雰囲気、ストーリーの良さ、豊富なアクションを失った。 死にゲーでなくなったかというと微妙。中盤過ぎたあたりからしっかり死にゲーしてくる。ただし、死にゲー特有の「慣れると楽しい」がなく、敵の火力が高いからただ死にやすいだけになっている。 音楽の良さだけは変わらず引き継がれている。(外部に委託しているからそりゃ変わらんわ)  前作がこってり濃いめオリジナル味噌ラーメンとすると、今作はあっさり薄めよくある塩ラーメンといった感じ。それならマグノリアでなくていいし、あっさり薄めの中に雑味が入っているためむしろ他作品のほうが良い。  前作やったことある人にはお勧めできない。 前作やってない人は今すぐそっちに行ってくれ。

[h2]作为银河城，勉强可玩；作为《ENDER LILIES》的正统续作，严重失格。[/h2]  我并不是没玩过前作就来锐评的玩家。《ENDER LILIES》我也是全成就，游玩时间 54 小时以上。前作当然有问题，而且问题非常明显：碰撞伤害、敌人位移逻辑、无敌时间、部分数值设计，都有不少粗糙甚至恶心人的地方。  但《LILIES》有一个极其罕见的长板： 它的气质太强了。  那种孤独、清冷、空灵、神圣，以及“满是污秽的世界中仍然保持洁净”的宿命感，在整个银河城品类里都非常少见。它不是单靠美术，也不是单靠音乐，而是美术、音乐、叙事、主角设定和世界观共同形成了 1+1+1>3 的效果。  而《ENDER MAGNOLIA》最大的问题是： 它优化掉了一些前作的毛刺，但也一起优化掉了前作最珍贵的灵魂。  [h3]先说优点[/h3]  音乐依然很好。Mili 的水平在线，Symbiosis 和 Dignity 都非常加分。虽然整体不如前作惊艳，也有不少地方存在“音乐很好，但和场景或 Boss 不太搭”的问题，但单独拿出来听，质量依然值得肯定。  B 结局结尾处，诺拉，也就是标题里的 Magnolia，对摧毁结界后自己可能无法继续维持魔力、终有一天会停止运行的看法是：“那就会和人类一样拥有寿命。” 这句话非常好，甚至可以说是整个游戏少数真正打中我、让我重新抬高评价的瞬间。  因为它终于把作品从“功能性科幻设定”拉回了 ENDER 系列该有的主题：有限性、牺牲、存在意义，以及纯洁之物终将消逝的悲伤。  可惜这样的瞬间太少了。  [h3]最大的问题，是气质崩塌[/h3]  《ENDER LILIES》的世界像一座墓园。玩家不是去帮助活人、修复社会、推进秩序，而是在一个已经死去的世界里，见证、净化、超度。 它的孤独感不是“NPC少”这么简单，而是整个世界已经停止运转，只剩下残响、遗骸、执念和守护。  而《ENDER MAGNOLIA》里活人太多了，NPC太多了，社会感太强了。村庄、居民、组织、阶层、工厂、管理体系，这些东西让世界从“末世神话”变成了“末世社会”。 这当然不是不能写，但它会直接冲淡前作最核心的神圣感和孤独感。  简单说： 《LILIES》像一场安静的葬礼。 《MAGNOLIA》像一次工业区参观。  它不是没有世界观，而是这个世界观太世俗，太功能化，太像一个普通 JRPG 背景板。迷雾、调律、人造生命体这些设定本来有机会形成新的精神核心，但游戏没有真正把它们做成和前作“污秽/洁净”同等级的宿命对立。  《LILIES》里，Lily 与污秽之间有一种天然的、不可调和的对立。 《MAGNOLIA》里，主角的调律能力和迷雾之间没有建立起同等强度的叙事纽带。 结果就是：前作是宿命，续作是任务。  [h3]剧情和角色塑造也很难让人满意[/h3]  主角 Lilac 长期处于“摄像头”状态。她会说话，也有立场，但缺少真正鲜明的人格弧光。游戏显然想塑造一个逐渐团结起来的队伍，但队伍关系没有真正成为叙事核心。  NPC数量很多，但真正有记忆点的很少。很多人类角色的存在不但没有增强世界，反而削弱了这个系列本该有的孤独感。  碎片叙事也不理想。文本不少，但真正能扩展世界、制造历史重量、让玩家产生想象空间的内容不多。很多时候不是“信息缺失产生引力”，而是“故意不说清楚”。 学魂系叙事最容易犯的错误就是：只学到了碎片，没有学到碎片背后的世界重量。  [h3]地图探索是我最不满意的部分之一[/h3]  本作地图整体太一本道，尤其后期问题非常明显。正常来说，银河城在后期获得足够多跑图能力后，应该进入一个空间重新展开的阶段：玩家开始用新能力重新理解旧地图，突破原本无法通过的路线，获得一种“我终于能支配这个世界”的感觉。  但《MAGNOLIA》后期，尤其是上层区，路线设计非常差，几乎全靠单向门和强行绕路来限制玩家。  这就很尴尬了。  一方面，本作明显参考了《银河战士》系列的能力设计，尤其是类似超级跑、超级跳这一类机动能力； 另一方面，它又不敢让这些能力真正改写地图结构。  结果就是：能力给了，但空间没有真正打开。 玩家机动性变强了，但探索感反而比前期还差。  这不是“玩家不喜欢跑图”，而是跑图应该带来探索、发现和空间理解，而不是被单向门反复恶心。  [h3]战斗系统也存在不少问题[/h3]  首先，取消碰撞伤害并不等于真正解决了前作的问题。 前作最恶心人的地方并不是“有碰撞伤害”本身，而是碰撞伤害、敌人后撤逻辑、玩家位移预期、无敌时间不足几者叠加，导致玩家经常因为系统预期不一致而吃到不合理伤害。  《MAGNOLIA》选择直接取消碰撞伤害，这当然让体验变顺了，但这更像是绕开问题，而不是正面解决问题。  其次，很多战斗机制没有真正进入核心循环。 破防系统尤其明显：存在感很强，但收益、触发节奏和战斗时间轴对不上，很多时候更像装饰性机制，而不是驱动玩家构筑和操作决策的核心系统。  技能手感方面也不全是进步。部分技能，尤其是弹反类技能，手感和反馈甚至不如前作。 动作更流畅了，但精确操作的成就感反而下降了。  [h3]数值和平衡问题更严重[/h3]  本作远程输出能力设计非常失衡。琉赛、约尔梵、罗娜这类远程组合的轮椅程度实在太夸张了。 高频、安全、覆盖好，还能和攻击回血遗物形成非常强的正反馈。  这导致一个很严重的问题： 近战模组显得像个小丑。  远程强不是问题，但远程强通常需要代价：弹药、能量、站桩风险、冷却窗口、功能偏科，至少要占一样。 但本作很多远程技能的代价明显不够，收益却非常稳定，于是系统很快收敛到少数最优解。  Build 看似变多了，但真正有意义的构筑选择并没有那么多。 一旦最优解太明显，玩家就不是在选择风格，而是在选择要不要自我折磨。  敌人血量和战斗节奏也依然有问题。很多战斗不是因为敌人设计有趣而变长，而是因为血厚。 TTK 过长，但决策密度不足，最后就会变成重复消耗。  [h3]完成度方面也并不如我预期[/h3]  这作全收集全成就，我大概 20 小时左右完成，而且这 20 小时里还包含大量单向门、绕路、后期跑图损耗。 对比前作，我在《ENDER LILIES》里至少玩了 54 小时。  当然，时长不能直接等于质量。 但问题是，《MAGNOLIA》既没有前作那种让我愿意长时间停留的气质，也没有用系统深度、地图复杂度或重复游玩价值补回来。  它不是“短小精悍”。 它是“核心体验还没充分展开，就已经结束了”。  更糟糕的是，一些回忆演出和美术资产质量比较粗糙。即使考虑到“回忆”本身应该带有模糊感，最终效果也很难说服我这是一个足够打磨过的成品质量。  [h3]所以我的结论是：[/h3]  《ENDER MAGNOLIA》不是完全不能玩。 它有不错的基础操作，便利性也比前作更高，音乐依然优秀，个别剧情瞬间也确实动人。  但它最大的问题是： 它没有理解《ENDER LILIES》为什么特别。  《LILIES》当然粗糙，甚至有很多不合理的地方。但它的气质极端、统一、不可替代。 《MAGNOLIA》则更安全、更现代、更工业化。但也更世俗、更平、更容易被消费完。  如果我要玩一款“更安全、更均衡、更工业化”的银河城，我未必非要选择《ENDER MAGNOLIA》。 而我选择 ENDER 系列，是因为我想要那种别处很难找到的孤独、清冷、空灵和神圣。  很遗憾，这一作没有给到。  最终评价：  [b]作为普通银河城：4.5/10。 作为《ENDER LILIES》的续作：不合格。[/b]  它解决了一些前作的问题， 但也一起解决掉了前作的灵魂。

Beautiful game.   Lots of variation in maps, puzzles, platforming challenges, and abilities to unlock. The game has a little bit of everything, all of which is nicely polished. I also appreciate the various difficulty options, which are a lot more creative than just "enemy health bar up or down".  The game's score is excellent from start to finish. So are the artstyle and the cutscene animations. Game balancing is overall solid, though I feel the enemies in the final section are a bit overtuned in terms of Health. But that and one particularly annoying boss are my only real complaints.  I had a good time from start to finish and can warmly recommend this

修改了许多一代玩起来令人难受的部分，但加入了更多。 有质量不错的回血饰品，但是小怪的伤害也能两三下打死自机，稍微浪一点被小怪打死也就在一瞬间，基本不能提供太多容错 后期boss更是左拳伤害高右拳高伤害，虽然游戏给了玩家轮椅选项，但也不能就因为这个把不用轮椅的游戏体验变成赤石吧？ 设计了冰冻\火焰\电击元素，然而整个游戏对这个系统感知最强的就是自己着火的时候，必须连续闪避才能灭火，配上部分小怪和boss的封走位设计就是闪了打断走位节奏死，不闪慢慢烧死； 部分boss多动症严重，很难跟上输出；  以上的缺点仍属于可接受范围，毕竟一代的战斗也是较为让人诟病的一点，个人最失望的是bgm的退步，本作boss战bgm几乎没有令人印象深刻的，场景bgm也只有部分比得上一代。也许一代bgm属于超常发挥，但作为续作无法与上一代比拟就是最大的罪过。

Вторая игра мне зашла, также как и первая. О графике, механиках и всём подобном судить не буду, т.к. это вкусовщина. Финальный босс был заметно сложнее Короля Скверны из первой части, но тех же эмоций от битвы с ним к сожелению игра не передала.  Дополнительно: На концовку Б, вышло гораздо больше игроков чем на концовку А.

Really fun game! I enjoy changing how you fight with homunculi to create a comfort playstyle. The combat and control feel more responsive compared to lilies. I hope to see more from this series in the future
Is veri guud, 100% is nice and fun to do.

The sequence surpasses the previous game in every aspect in gameplay and the soundtracks are beautifully arranged as well. However, the lively background, people here and there moving around and actually living allies frequently interacting with Lilac unintentionally diminish the feeling of player about the deadly context of the game. The despairing atmosphere with creepy mutated enemies still makes Ender Lilies more memorable than the sequence. Another downside is that Ender Magnolia gives you dash and double jump simultaneously at the very beginning of the game. This unnecessary generosity negatively effects the exploring experience because these upgrades are fundamentally essential in metroidvania regardless of how unique the other upgrades are. The game overall still is a worth-play, the playstyle is varied and, especially, the parry is so much more useful, i previously hated the parry of Ender Lilies because of how it is attached to the dash, but in this game i abused it so much that the final boss was not really challenging. I also like the map improvements, while Ender Lilies makes players crawl around blind boxes, this game has a very clear map, even background details are precisely printed on it. Really looking forward to more Ender games

Отличный представитель жанра метроидваний. Отличная боевая система, красивый сюжет, хотя между двумя частями он очень схож. Видно приличное развитие между частями. Удобное разделение на экраны на карте с указанием зачищено или что-то ещё там есть.

玩完一代后紧接着玩的二代，手感上确实好了不少[玩了二代后就真的回不去了]，技能，装扮种类确实多了很多，地图也确实没让我迷路了（并且在继承一代优良传统[完全探索后标记]的基础上，把自定义地图标记加上去了[虽然这代不迷路反而不需要这个了]），镰刀好轮椅(怪在天上飞我在地上/隔墙打有没有懂的)，技能好轮椅，纯战斗爽游戏。体验下来唯一的槽点是bgm做的没一代好了（点名表扬一代的魔女之森，悬崖村）没有让人印象深刻的神曲了（当然也可能和性能太强探图太快有关系就是了))，游戏总体而言很“中庸”，我的意思不是说不好，是想表达它其实是一个合格的续作，当然，也就合格而已（当然也很棒了）。小技巧：和一代一样，本作依然可以通过跳跃/冲刺重置连击段数，从而达到延长滞空时间的效果（更重要的是这作二段跳拿的特别早），面对一些很长的空中距离，你可以通过：起跳→边攻击边移动直至连击段数耗尽→冲刺→继续连击+移动→二段跳→继续连击+移动→冲刺（跳跃重置冲刺）→继续连击+移动  这样的方式移动相当长一段横向距离（前期也能把收集做个七七八八），中期配上7号的雪之型（向上位移的攻击技能）以及月之型（水平冲刺的攻击技能），可以打出更逆天的位移效果，真的玩多了自己都会笑出来。我探图就带镰刀，雪之型，嫌麻烦带个猫头鹰，再带个罗娜(能多个无敌回血的sp技能增加容错)，简直是一路平推走哪杀哪，15小时初见全成就真是轻轻又松松啊，如果喜欢这个系列推荐先玩一代再玩二代，不然倒过来玩一是剧情对不上[当然了不看剧情也行]，二是手感会不习惯，一代平A手感懂的都懂好吧，墙裂推荐！

This game improved upon everything that Ender Lilies had in spades. The exploration was extremely fun rewarding. The homunculus abilities were fantastic and allowed for different playstyles depending on what you like. Not to mention that the game's visuals are amazing. I strongly recommend this to anyone who enjoyed Ender Lilies or enjoys Metroidvanias in general.
比一代强了非常多
7/10 相较于莉莉确实改善了很多游玩上的痛点，比如能力锁安排，地图设计和战斗节奏，虽然不能说是做的很好，至少体验好多了。但是前作的卖点剧情美术音乐却有些倒退，尤其是剧情，整体看来就是很平淡很平淡，甚至和整体美术风格都有些格格不入，就像是把各种设定融合起来最后写了个日轻厕纸王道冒险，属实是很退步了
整体流程比较像白开水，音乐方面还是可圈可点的。战斗感觉制作组有一些想法，但想法不多，一半的机制和武器都是鸡肋
挺好玩的，喜欢一代的话，直接玩就好，同类型的游戏不多了

[h1] Комфорт [/h1] И первую часть, и сиквел ценю за ощущение комфортного приключения.   Никто у меня ничего не отбирает со смертью, телепорты работают почти с начала и бесплатно, в любой момент можно вернуться к последнему посещенному по одной кнопке, нет вонючей беготни до босса (так можно, MIO, прикинь), карта показывает куда ты ещё не залез, короче - [strike] аниме [/strike] кайф.  [h2] Карта [/h2] Её, наконец-то, сделали читабельной, почти идеально, даже метки не нужны. Если помните карту из прошлой игры, это небо и земля.  [b] Почти [/b] идеально, потому что из серого неизвестного она окрашивается в зачищенный синий (приятно, спасибо), но по не всегда очевидной логике. Да и можно было бы разные локации красить в разные цвета, чтобы по взгляду на цвет понимать, что это за локация, а не по форме вспоминать. Но это ладно, придирки.   Хотя я однажды просто на ней заблудился. Карте не хватает вот этого маркера, который где-то там висит и указывает тебе примерное направление поиска.  Вместо него здесь вот эта максимально размытая ерунда в духе «найди проход в верхний сектор», что на человеческом означает «исследуй карту». Спасибо, интересная мысль. И я даже не один такой, в моменте от безысходности я полез смотреть что делать и человек ровно в том же месте носился час, гадая, куда теперь идти.  Этому блужданию и сайд квесты чуть способствуют, потому что некоторые персонажи тут говорят на Миядзаки-невнятном диалекте и в трактовке вообще не сложно ошибиться.  Тем не менее, некий указатель пути на карте действительно был, да. Но чтобы его заметить, интуитивно, так сказать, ну, я не знаю кем надо быть.  Но в целом, в целом, путешествовать всё равно приятно. Поток, всё такое.  [h2] Как и драться. [/h2]  Боёвка под стать остальной игре не требует никаких хардкорных навыков. Она комфортная. Тем не менее врагов много и они разные, поэтому пользоваться всеми доступными кнопками всё ещё эффективно. Больше того, всё честно, имею ввиду - все движения точны, врагов всегда видно и они даже расставлены так, что случайно хоть на кого-то налететь практически невозможно. Честно говоря, не помню первую часть в подробностях, но по ощущениям вторая сделала лучше то, что было хорошо. Хотя, в конце разработчики чуть-чуть не удержались и навалили таки толстых уроновых губок. Ну, такое, но для финальной части терпимо, наверно.  Какие-то сильно странные приколы тоже в наличии. Из игры, например, практически пропали арены, где надо убить мобов, что гуд, но добавили парочку зон, где одного и того же мини босса надо убивать типа 3 или 4 раза подряд практически, просто, чтобы дальше пройти. За это ничего не дают, только отнимают время. Игра и так не короткая, карта не маленькая, зачем ещё нужна была такая дополнительная резина - вопрос.   Боссы в целом норм, все проходимы, на всё есть контратака, хотя и последний, которого, по традиции, для двух концовок надо убить дважды, на мой взгляд, кусается неоправданно больно. Он ещё и жирный, скотина, но он последний, имеет право (наверно). И босс на заводе роботов тоже черт, ящитаю.  Вообще, боёвка понравилась, но она всё ещё близка к закликиванию. Это не то чтобы минус, просто вот она такая. Требует скорее внимательности, а не скилла. Тут врагов даже через стены бить можно.   Музыка хороша, не вся, но некоторые мотивы весьма приятные.  [b] В итоге,[/b] если любите жанр, то игру стоит пройти. Если любите первую, то вторую надо пройти. Игра - классное приключение, не задушенное всякой ху*той хардко-ко-кора ради, а цельное, не идеальное, но цельное, продуманное и комфортное приключение.   Проходил, кстати, на Деке и там она отлично играется. Такая история.
That's a very good sequel of the first game. Not better, not bigger, just another great piece from this universe
高不成低不就吧，关卡设计和战斗都一般，难也就是后期数值膨胀导致的容错下降，故事没仔细看，这个家族那个血统的真不如看别人做的剧情解析，重玩价值非常低，我这个时长再看攻略多打一两个小时也就全收集了，音乐美术确实好，但是真的撑不起整个游戏，为了音乐美术买游戏本身也是个伪命题，因为游戏要足够好玩才能让大部分人坚持着把音乐美术体验完。 好评只是因为它不烂，而不是有多好。

Ender Magnolia to sequel bardzo udanej metroidvanii - Ender Lillies. Choć poprzednik nie był doskonałą grą, to wyróżniało go parę rzeczy jak koncept atakowania tylko za pomocą przyzywanych duchów czy piękne utwory muzyczne wykonywane głównie z wykorzystaniem pianina. Ender Magnolia jest kontynuacją próbującą zachować wszystko co było dobre w jedynce i na jej podstawie ulepszyć to co wymagało poprawy. Czy to się twórcom udało? Moim zdaniem (w ogromnej większości) tak. Ender Magnolia to świetna metroidvania, tak samo dobra lub lepsza od poprzedniczki w prawie każdym aspekcie.  [h2]Zmiany i ulepszenia w systemie walki[/h2]Walka nadal opiera się na przyzywaniu duchów wojowników, którzy wykonują za nas ataki swoim orężem/czarami. Nie ma tutaj większych rewolucji i podobnie jak w jedynce ma to zabieg głównie kosmetyczny, bo w praktyce to tak jakbyśmy po prostu korzystali z różnych broni. Na pierwszy rzut oka nie ma tutaj ich aż tak dużo jak w EL - towarzyszy nam łącznie jedynie 9 "duchów". Każdy z nich ma za to 3 różne ataki, więc rzeczywista łączna ilość broni to aż 30 (o kilka więcej niż w jedynce). Ataki te dzielą się na kategorie primary (główne źródło obrażeń), cooldown (po każdym użyciu musimy czekać aż się odnowi i możemy ich używać w trakcie innych ataków), pressure (ataki dystansowe) oraz auto (przyzwany summon pasywnie atakuje bez naszej ingerencji). Ten podział zdolności stanowi świetną wskazówkę do tego jakie buildy robić (możemy mieć ekwipowane jednocześnie max 4 bronie), a same zdolności naszych towarzyszy są bardzo różnorodne. Mały minus tylko za to, że podobnie jak w jedynce pewne zdolności są wyraźnie mniej przydatne od swoich alternatyw.  W porównaniu do Ender Lillies, movement naszej postaci uległ poprawie - miałem wrażenie że wyraźnie szybciej odzyskujemy kontrolę po wykonywaniu ataków, a także jestem pewien że atakowanie w powietrzu przesuwa nas o znacznie większe odległości niż w jedynce. Same przejścia pomiędzy ruchem, a animacjami ataków wydały mi się też bardziej płynne. Choć nie przeszkadzało mi to jakoś strasznie w EL, to zdecydowanie nie mam nic przeciwko usunięciu obrażeń przy dotykaniu wrogów dopóki nie wykonają jakiegoś ataku.  [h2]Ułatwienia i uprzyjemnienie eksploracji[/h2]Podobnie jak w jedynce, stopniowo (w metroidvaniowym stylu) odblokowujemy nowe zdolności pozwalające nam na dostęp do nowych obszarów. Aspekt eksploracji nadal jest bardzo dobry, zachęcający do zaglądania w każdy kąt, a dowolność wyboru następnych lokacji nawet jeszcze większa. Praktycznie na każdym etapie gry miałem możliwość udania się do jednego z 2-3 obszarów i gra co chwila ujawniała przede mną nowe regiony.  Co ważniejsze jednak, gra naprawia główny grzech jedynki, czyli przesadnie dużo backtrackingu do poprzednich obszarów. Przy pierwszym przechodzeniu EL była to zdecydowanie rzecz która najbardziej mi się nie podobała i bliżej końca gry miałem już trochę dość wracania do starych lokacji, żeby sprawdzić czy nowa zdolność umożliwi mi odblokowanie przejścia czy nie. W EM opcjonalnych przedmiotów nadal jest sporo, ale gra nie zawala nas znacznikami o nich od samego początku gry. W dodatku wymagają one najczęściej ulepszeń, które już zdobyliśmy w ostatnim czasie, albo zdobędziemy niedługo. A nawet, gdy w późniejszych etapach pojawiają się przeszkody, których nie będziemy mogli przez jakiś czas zniszczyć, to od razu będziemy to wiedzieć. Mapa pokazująca znacznie więcej niż w części pierwszej - mamy dokładniejszy layout każdej lokacji, a także ikonki reprezentujące przedmioty do podniesienia czy rodzaj przeszkody blokującej przejście. Dzięki tym wszystkim oznaczeniom możemy od razu zobaczyć w których odwiedzonych przez nas miejscach da się użyć nowo zdobytej zdolności, nawet jeśli zapomnieliśmy o którymś z tych miejsc.  [h2]Głębszy system rozwoju postaci[/h2]O ile w jedynce mogliśmy tylko ulepszać nasze "bronie" oraz dostawać pasywne wzmocnienia wraz z zyskiwaniem expa, tutaj oprócz tego mamy system ekwipunku w którym zakładamy na naszą postać części wyposażenia o różnych statystykach. Część tego ekwipunku zdobywamy eksplorując otoczenie, część kupujemy w sklepie który jest dostępny w mieście. Niedaleko tego sklepu jest także możliwość ulepszania "Hollow Knightowych" relikwii zużywających sloty. Ciekawą rzeczą jest wprowadzenie status effectów - burn, shock oraz freeze, które możemy nałożyć na wrogów a także, które sami możemy dostać od przeciwników. W grze jest trochę zdolności nakładających te efekty, a także relikwii je wzmacniających, dzięki czemu można ułożyć sobie pod nie build.  [h2]Audiowizualnie[/h2]Ender Lillies było na prawdę śliczną grą z charakterystycznymi, bardzo ładnymi designami naszych grywalnych postaci, a także wszelkich wrogów. Ender Magnolia delikatnie ten artstyle zmienia, ale nadal zachowuje podobną stylistykę i ją dodatkowo ulepsza. Animacje, a także modele postaci posiadają wyraźnie więcej szczegółów. Na szczególną pochwalę zasługują efekty różnych czarów/ciosów, które są tutaj znacznie bardziej różnorodne i też bardzo ładnie przedstawione.  Jeśli chodzi o soundtrack to kwestia tego, który jest lepszy jest czysto subiektywna. Muzykę do Ender Magnolia skomponował ten sam twórca, jednak (pewnie z uwagi na odmienną warstwę fabularną) znacząco się ona różni od tej z jedynki. Ogólnie muszę przyznać, że muzyka jest zdecydowanie na plus, dobrze pasuje do poszczególnych lokacji, posiada parę fenomenalnych kawałków, a także jest bardziej różnorodna niż w jedynce.  [h2]Znacząco inne podejście do fabuły[/h2]O ile gameplay obył się bez większych zmian, po prostu poprawiając to co było już dobre, to konstrukcja i styl fabuły jak i świata uległy znaczącym zmianom. Tym razem nie mamy do czynienia ze światem który już obumarł, lecz z takim który nadal się jakoś trzyma i jest wypełniony NPC-ami. Protagonistka nie jest też niema i ma własny charakter, podobnie jak każdy z naszych przywołańców. Sam świat choć nadal post apokaliptyczny i będący w tym samym uniwersum co jedynka (tylko kilkadziesiąt lat później), nie jest zwyczajnym fantasy. Widoczne są tu elementy steampunku, m.in. w lokacjach jakie odwiedzamy (fabryki itp.) czy w przeróżnych rodzajach zmechanizowanych przeciwników z jakimi się mierzymy.  Dzięki tym zmianom, historia w Magnoli pozostawia za sobą wyraźnie inne odczucia niż ta z Lillies, co nie każdemu może się spodobać. Choć ogólnie jest to dobrze napisana opowieść i bardzo podobały mi się postacie, to personalnie trochę bardziej wolę fabułę z jedynki, podobnie zresztą jak i ponury, melancholijny klimat tamtego świata. Warto też wspomnieć, że podobnie jak w jedynce, twórcom zabrakło budżetu na kwestie dźwiękowe i wszystkie dialogi musimy czytać z napisów.  [h2]Podsumowanie[/h2]Ogólnie, Ender Magnolia opiera się na tych samych podstawach co Ender Lillies i stanowi naturalne rozwinięcie koncepcji gameplay'u. Z pewnością spodoba się więc każdemu fanowi jedynki (a szczególnie tym, którzy regularnie się gubili w tamtej grze). Gameplay był dla mnie bardzo wciągający, a oprawa audiowizualna była bardzo miła w odbiorze. Chociaż fabularnie gra dość mocno odchodzi od klimatów jedynki, to nie mogę zaprzeczyć że jest to mimo wszystko zupełnie nowa i bardzo ciekawa historia. Jeśli ktoś jest fanem metroidvanii, a nie miał do czynienia z Ender Lillies to polecam zapoznanie się najpierw właśnie z nią. W ostateczności jednak można też zacząć od tej gry - nie jest to bezpośredni sequel i praktycznie całą fabułę można zrozumieć bez znajomości poprzedniego tytułu (choć ominie Cię wtedy trochę nawiązań). Magnolia wydała mi się zdecydowanie trudniejszą grą niż Lillies, aczkolwiek przechodziłem ją z modyfikatorami odpowiadającymi poziomowi Hard, a w jedynce z racji braku oficjalnych poziomów trudności, nic nie zmieniałem. Po wyczyszczeniu całej mapy i wbiciu sekretnego true endingu miałem na liczniku w grze 23 godziny, a na Steam 25 (czyli podobnie co w przypadku mojego pierwszego przejścia Ender Lillies).

Personally, I think it's not as good as the previous Ender Lilies, but it is good, so don't get me wrong about that.  Ender Magnolia is a game where you play as a duo of defender and little girl. Yeah, basically epic warrior protects the chosen loli. On one hand, I am kinda tired of this trope. On the other hand, too many other products out there take such cute characters and degrade them into 'cute' characters. It's happened a bunch, but thankfully I don't recall any such pandering in this game nor anything like it.  As for gameplay, it's another 2D soulslike metroidvania game with complex platforming techniques and an emphasis on explorations and boss fights. Some of the game's encounters are poorly balanced, and some are outright bad. Personally, you shouldn't feel the need to explore every nook and cranny just to have enough HP not to die so fast.   Look, I know people say they love a good challenge, but that's not what they mean. They mean they want a MEANINGFUL challenge. Let's compare this difficulty to Dark Souls 1, where contrary to common claims, enemies didn't kill you in one or two hits. It took three, four, or more hits depending on your equipment. Only a handful of situations could drain you down rapidfire, and those sections either required discretion and stealth, such as with the red dragon on the bridge, or the Bed of Chaos, which is generally considered to be the worst boss fight of the game. Furthermore, the moves that did have high damage potential among bosses were either spectacles with plenty of time for you to avoid, they were rarely used, or some other detail that made good design sense. However, now we live in the age of Elden Ring, where difficulty is so absurd that it borders on obscenity. Ender Magnolia suffers from this problem. And no, giving players a difficulty slider option to fix the bad design choices does not make these bad design choices go away, it merely hides them.  Difficulty imbalance was a major concern for me in the original title, and now it returns the same. It's nauseating. Biggers studios can get away with this crap, especially Fromsoft because they have inspired our faith, but this is small potatoes in comparison.

如果上一作Ender Lilies满分10分我给9分，这一作我只能给到7-7.5分。 两句话简评就是：“一想到前作是充满了无边的孤独感的冒险体验，在绝望中挣扎出一片生机，最后落得一个半身不遂；而这一作合家欢乐，应有尽有就十分的出戏。我怀着做好牺牲准备的悲壮心情点开了作为续作的这个游戏，玩下来是合家欢，我就很难给这个续作多高的评价。” 首先说优点： 画风延续上一作，依旧十分惊艳，给到10分。 世界观也是相同的，因此还有旧作的情怀加持。 游戏性方面：堆怪问题仍有存在，但是大幅改善。去掉了碰撞受伤，这个大赞。新增了很多轮椅技能，数值过于逆天，玩下来会发现近战平A流就是最弱的流派，高风险低收益，除了有助于跳跳乐左脚踩右脚升空没什么用。但是这一作的跳跳乐其实设计的很克制，十分少，还是比较友好的。难度也可以调整了，更适合普通玩家上手。游戏性真的大大提升了。莉莉的游戏性给7分的话，这一作给9分不过分。 接下来说缺点： 前作最令人印象深刻的优点反而没了，那就是剧情+画面+音乐+优秀的角色塑造所整体带来的沉浸感，代入感。还记得我打完莉莉的时候，玉玉了一整个星期没缓过来，但是这一作我打完最终BOSS只会气笑了。 BOSS的设计和音乐的缺失就是最最让我失望的点。一代最令人眼前一亮的就是，二阶段乃至三阶段的boss，不仅有体型/设计上的整体视觉变化，招式变化，而且音乐也会对应的变化。但是这一作除了前期的医官鸟，后期的BOSS均赶工痕迹明显，还有本应有却没有的BOSS战（如轮椅妹），确实是让人觉得没有整体做好就端了上来，十分的可惜。 音乐虽然也有喜欢的BGM，两代下来，制作组真的给Mili磕一个（她们的曲子真的太神了，其他游戏的也是）。但是这一作大概是钱没给到位吧，精良曲子的数量跟不上了（我总不可能去质疑Mili做不出精致曲子吧）。 剧情的讲述十分的支离破碎，不去琢磨剧情甚至让人难以理解基础框架。角色的塑造也十分的无力和片面，两者结合让人不知道这里边的人都在干嘛。大概就是有种“他们为什么要打架”的感觉。 另外一个影响氛围感的还是这一作实在太阳间了吧（），难度低，再加上剧情云里雾里，角色塑造不够立体， 活人也多，不仅活人多正常人也多，还有你露神这种不应该出现在这种阴郁世界观里的神级的BUG角色，整体就是合家欢。 只能说制作组对产品的定位不是很明确，再加上赶工明显，虽有优化改进的地方，但抛弃了原先最大的优点，太可惜了。希望下一作可以做好，期待下一作。

[h2]世界或许如同地狱，但我们仍将生存且抗争到底[/h2] [h3]9/10[/h3] 终焉之玛格诺利亚——终焉之莉莉丝的续作。它向我们传达出的精神与前作并无二致，我想可以用“绝望中绽放的花朵”来概括。  在这一次的旅途中，我们跟随莱拉克的视角，逐步拼凑出烟尘之国完整的记忆，并在最后揭露真相。这一切太过残忍，没有谁有去背负那污秽与变异的责任，首席调律师尤兰没有，莉莉娅没有，莱拉克没有，诺拉没有，吉尔罗伊没有，米利厄斯与弗罗斯特家族的任何人也都不应该有。在前作中，背负与救赎的精神内核时刻体现，但在这一作里，我想它得到了升华。  Magnolia，意为木兰，花语——高尚的灵魂。或许在游戏的开头，胸口深深插着剑，跪地不起的诺拉便暗示了一切。这一场景明显neta了《渎神》中主角拔出“孽刃”的场面，苦痛奇迹或许在诺拉身上也是完全适用的；由死亡之雨诞下的苦痛，没有谁需要背负了。让我们创造奇迹吧——用那高尚的灵魂。  音乐和美术方面依旧顶级，我还是那么爱听Mili！本作靠背景与乐句的结合依旧让我感到这个世界是宏大、孤寂的，却又让我感到绝境中的希望。  难度适中，引导好，开图爽，收集爽。点亮地图的成就感伴随游戏始终。真的是能一口气玩完的，不会无聊。  不管是类银老手还是想入坑的都推荐游玩。

I had hoped to enjoy Ender Magnolia, though I was unsure if I would given I am critical of Ender Lilies, but I also felt like that game had the foundation for something I could genuinely enjoy if certain design issues were improved. This sequel does address some of those criticisms in meaningful ways, yet somehow the overall experience still failed to hook me. While I can recognize improvements mechanically, the game as a whole felt strangely flat and unmemorable to play.  The most immediately noticeable improvement is combat usability. Contact damage has been removed, which instantly makes movement and positioning feel less irritating during encounters. Abilities also no longer have limited uses, meaning players are encouraged to experiment more freely instead of hoarding attacks. These are meaningful changes that improve the game on a mechanical level and make the overall experience less restrictive.  Unfortunately, the core gameplay surrounding those improvements feels oddly flat. Combat never developed into something exciting for me because enemy health pools become bloated fairly early into the game. Encounters start dragging on as enemies simply absorb too much damage for too long. This was one of my major criticisms of the prior game as well, and while some systems are cleaner here, the underlying pacing problem remains.  Equipment is another major disappointment. Most gear bonuses are incredibly passive and uninteresting, usually amounting to small percentage boosts like increasing a specific action by 10% to 20%. It feels like a huge missed opportunity. A system like this could have introduced meaningful build variety or dramatically altered how the player approaches combat, but instead it mostly feels like incremental stat padding with little excitement behind it.  Movement also feels overly floaty, which negatively impacts both combat and platforming. Attacks and jumps lack any character weight, and nothing about traversal or combat ever clicked into a satisfying rhythm for me. Even after several hours, the movement never felt fun.  The presentation is visually appealing. Areas look nice in the moment, yet very little stood out afterward. The music follows a similar pattern. Tracks sound good initially, but because the same looping songs repeat constantly within each area, they eventually became grating instead of atmospheric.  Boss quality is also inconsistent. Some bosses are surprisingly easy and can be defeated on a first attempt with little resistance, while others suddenly become far more mechanically demanding and complex. The difficulty curve feels uneven and it doesn’t feel like they were tested appropriately, as difficulty spikes feel random and fights feel uneven mechanically.  One feature I do genuinely appreciate is the ability to skip cutscenes. More games should include this by default, especially in genres that encourage replayability or repeated attempts after death.  I would not call the game bad. There is clear craftsmanship here, and I can understand why fans of the first game may enjoy the direction it takes. But despite putting hours into it hoping the experience would eventually evolve into something more engaging, it never did for me. Even though it fixes several problems I had with the original, it also failed to give me much to latch onto emotionally or mechanically, which ultimately made it a miss for me overall.

Praticamente uma extensão do primeiro jogo, mas melhor em inúmeros aspectos, porém nem tão bom em outros (ao menos pro meu gosto).   Comparado a Ender Lilies, Magnolia tem bem mais variedade em relação a forma como você pode jogar o jogo com as diferentes possibilidades de builds que você pode criar pro seu personagem usando o sistema de artefatos e equipamentos, sem falar das skills, cada personagem possui 3 skills e são 10 personagens, tem bastante customização na questão da gameplay e isso é algo que gostei bastante na parte da gameplay, de certo modo isso até deixa o jogo mais fácil que o primeiro, achei a maioria dos bosses fáceis de enfrentar, vão haver mortes, mas nada que um pouco de adaptação não resolva, acho que o jogo sabe disso compensando no tanto de dano que você toma a cada porrada que você leva, também achei o design de combate e mecânica de alguns bosses inferiores ao primeiro, mas num geral ainda tá bom pro gênero.  No entanto, apesar de lindo visualmente eu não acho Magnolia melhor que Lilies nessa parte, essa parte é uma questão de gosto, mas acredito que Lilies tinha uma melhor direção de arte, o mesmo se aplica a história do jogo pra mim, não consegui me importar tanto com a história do Magnolia até chegar quase que na metade do jogo, ainda é uma história extremamente interessante, com muito espaço pra teoria, mas diferente da história de Ender Lilies eu não fiquei com a sensação de querer saber mais sobre os mistérios daquele lugar, no Magnolia a história é muito melhor contada no entanto, existem vários npcs importantes e cada área do jogo explica e conecta a história de uma com a outra, o Lilies tinha uma boa história também mas era bem mais escassa de detalhes.  O jogo é muito bom, quem curtiu o primeiro vai curtir o segundo e quem curte Metroidvania vai curtir jogar ambos.

Намного более цельная, качественная и проработанная игра по сравнению с первой частью. С самого начала меня просто восхитил новый UI, особенно на фоне первой части. Добавили огромное количество квалити оф лайф механик, билдостроение стало заметно глубже, но при этом не превратилось в излишне комплексное. Есть как мощные способности, которые требуют от игрока больше мастерства, так и более простые, но менее эффективные. Так же статусные эффекты, но они скорее как второстепенные вещи, так как и без подстраивания все боссы легко побеждаются В целом игра мне очень понравилась. За 16 часов она ни разу не показалась затянутой, в отличие от первой части, где к концу уже сильно утомляла Единственный минус (скорее субъективный) игра довольно казуальная. Даже на высокой сложности (которую ещё нужно найти в настройках сидя на скамье) просто увеличивается входящий урон на ~1.5–1.7 раза, и всё. Секретки тоже часто очень очевидные. Только одну пришлось искать через интерактивную карту на стороннем сайте, хотя потом оказалось, что она довольно простая, я просто оказался слепым пнём Боссы, геймдизайн и левелдизайн заметно улучшились. Чувствуется, что разработчики реально постарались Первую часть проходить необязательно. игра будет хорош как и отдельный продукт. Если очень хочется, будет чуть приятнее из-за отсылок и лора, но сюжет почти не связан с оригиналом (всего 1–3 персонажа + небольшие упоминания). Как самостоятельная игра она тоже отлично заходит Сюжет тоже стал лучше. НПС очень пошли игре на пользу - в первой части я уже реально устал ходить в одиночестве и общаться только с духами как сумасшедший друид. Музыка приятная, без странных экспериментов и криков в стиле Fear & Hunger, как было в первой части одних локациях.. там это был простоо ад. за 16.2 часа прошел на платину

优点： 1.有了细致的地图描绘，而且延续上一代收集全区域会显示蓝色，全收集较友好。 2.取消了碰撞伤害。 3.钩爪不是一弹一弹的而是直接固定操作稍微轻松了一些。 缺点（以下包含较多与一代相比较的拉踩）： 1.我极度不满意的剧情部分，说实话一代即使这手感很烂极致堆怪我也愿意二刷三刷，因为整个地图与世界真的融合的很好！你一代cg简洁明了的体现了这个人物，丰富的文本补充了世界观与人物形象，最后mili优美的音乐与地图的场景叙事配合更让人印象深刻。所有boss我现在都能说出他们的剧情，体会他们的不甘悔恨与对某人某事的爱，而二代呢？我底层探完除了鸟哥其他人什么故事我压根没什么印象，过场cg图又慢又糊，然后其中一堆人名但又不知道是谁，我看完就感觉人工生命体被弄的很惨其他都没了，到了二层勉强懂了一点人物关系，但还是很烂，我甚至打完各种结局只有一种就这？垃圾的剧情导致与诺拉也没什么陪伴感，拯救了世界也没什么感动，甚至救对主角极其重要的莉莉丝我也无法感同身受的着急。 2.boss设计。二代没有一个boss我是印象深刻喜欢的。一代修女姐姐和狼叔这爽快而华丽的打斗二代完全没有。可能因为一代明确区分了剧情主要boss与小怪boss，导致主boss的强爽与小怪boss的路边完全不矛盾。而二代感觉整体都充斥着又強又菜的感觉，强指的是一个伤害打掉我半条血的力大砖飞，菜指的是真的出招就那么三四个，大部分都看的明白，但这厚重的闪避却使得躲起来超憋屈，更何况其中几个多动症真的是跑上跑下的打不到更憋屈了。 3.在上分地图优点的基础上是什么让你给了完整地图就整出传送这种垃圾的地图切换方式的？进了一个场景神奇的三个传送点三选一选了到另一个地图回不来，有时候按错一个点意味着你要从头的传送点再开始真的恶心啊！魔女学院也恶心真的不理解了。 4.我打全成就却被吞成就了而且我不想再吃一遍了必须给个差评。

This game is in my top three favorite MVs. It improved on all the flaws of the first game, making it an outstanding game.   Main drawbacks of Ender Lilies: 1. Map system - fixed in Magnolia 2. Difficulty level - the game is easier and there's an easy mode 3. Contact damage - a terrible thing, luckily it's not in Magnolia  Now about Magnolia:  Positives: Combat - the combat is great, using spirits is fun, leveling them up, and choosing different attacks - there are so many combinations that you can create your own build and defeat enemies however you want. There are also plenty of relics, totems, and bracelets to buy and equip - you can really fight however you want.  Exploration - great, with the fixed map system where you can see everything + set your own markers, and thanks to the clear marking of locations that haven't been cleared, plus plenty of collectibles and fake walls - all of this makes exploration great, and I cleared every region, all maps (all maps blue, so all maps 100%), which doesn't happen often for me.   Abilities - There are plenty of them, they're in sensible places (mainly after bosses), they have a nice effect on movement and exploration, and double jump/dash are obtained very early, as they should be.  Atmosphere and story - In my opinion, it's better than in the first game. It has great characters, and uncovering the story is interesting, interesting enough that I got a true ending in this game, which I don't do often.  Soundtrack - This is the first time in a metrodvania that I listened to the OST outside of the game. This makes the OST truly brilliant, and I could listen to some of the themes forever, so I purposely traveled to these places and didn't rush through them.  Difficulty level - I played the game on easy, and I think it's an accessible difficulty level. The regular enemies aren't as tough as in the first game, and the bosses aren't either. (Back then, the six bosses were terrible), but here, I only got stuck with two for a while. Velgrynd and the final fight with Gilroy are bosses that should have been easier. A small downside for those two; I beat the rest in 1, 2, and 3 tries (a lot of bosses and mini-bosses 1st tried).  Platforming - there are a few more difficult areas that killed me multiple times, but overall, this game has a very accessible level of platforming (not hard), and using all abilities is easy.  Length and amount of content - the game is longer than the first one, has more locations, takes longer to complete, and Steam calculates it strangely. I didn't play this game for 45 hours, but it took me about 30-35 hours to complete it slowly and calmly.  Negatives: None  In summary, this is an outstanding metroidvania, 9/10 at least.
